📖 English

Use of Nouns

Learn about people, places, things, and ideas - the naming words of English!

📚 Chapter 1 ⏱️ 8 min read

Unit 1: Use of Nouns

A noun is a naming word. It names a person, place, animal, thing, or idea. Without nouns, we cannot name anything around us!

Example: boy, Kathmandu, happiness, table, school

Types of Nouns

Let us explore the five main families of nouns.

1. Proper Nouns – Specific Names

Proper nouns name a particular person, place, or thing. They always begin with a capital letter.

Proper NounType
RamPerson
NepalCountry
KathmanduCity
SundayDay
JanuaryMonth

Example in a sentence: Ram lives in Kathmandu.

2. Common Nouns – General Names

Common nouns name any ordinary person, place, or thing. They do not need a capital letter (unless starting a sentence).

Common NounType
boyPerson
cityPlace
schoolThing
monthTime
dayTime

Example in a sentence: The boy goes to school.

3. Collective Nouns – Names of Groups

Collective nouns name a group of people, animals, or things as one unit.

Collective NounGroup of
teamplayers
armysoldiers
classstudents
flockbirds
herdcattle
swarmbees

Example in a sentence: The team won the match.

4. Material Nouns – Names of Substances

Material nouns name the substance or material something is made from.

Material NounUsed for
goldjewellery
waterdrinking
woodfurniture
cottonclothes
plasticbottles
irontools

Example in a sentence: The ring is made of gold.

5. Abstract Nouns – Names of Qualities or Ideas

Abstract nouns name things we cannot see or touch – feelings, ideas, and qualities.

Abstract NounType
honestyquality
braveryquality
happinessfeeling
lovefeeling
freedomidea
childhoodstage of life

Example in a sentence: Honesty is the best policy.

💡 Tip: If you cannot touch it or see it, it is probably an abstract noun!

Quick Check: Types of Nouns

SentenceNounType
Nepal is beautiful.NepalProper
The boy is reading.boyCommon
The army marched forward.armyCollective
Drink water every day.waterMaterial
Bravery saved the day.braveryAbstract

Other Important Uses of Nouns

Singular and Plural Nouns

Nouns can be singular (one) or plural (more than one).

Rules for Making Plurals

RuleSingularPlural
Add -sbookbooks
Add -es (words ending in s, sh, ch, x, o)bus, box, mangobuses, boxes, mangoes
Change y to i and add -es (consonant + y)baby, citybabies, cities
Add -s (vowel + y)boy, dayboys, days
Change f/fe to v and add -esleaf, knifeleaves, knives
Irregular (change inside word)child, tooth, manchildren, teeth, men
Same formsheep, deersheep, deer

Examples in sentences:

  • One child is playing. (singular)
  • Two children are playing. (plural)

Countable and Uncountable Nouns

Countable nouns can be counted (one apple, two apples).
Uncountable nouns cannot be easily counted (milk, rice, water).

Countable Nouns ✅Uncountable Nouns ❌
apple, pen, chair, bookmilk, rice, sugar, water
Can use a/an (a pen)Cannot use a/an (a milk)
Can be plural (pens)Cannot be plural (milks)
Use many/few (many books)Use much/little (much water)

Examples:

  • Countable: How many apples? – Three apples.
  • Uncountable: How much milk? – One glass of milk.

Gender of Nouns

Nouns can show if a person or animal is male, female, or neither.

Masculine (Male)Feminine (Female)Neuter (No gender)
kingqueentable
boygirlbook
lionlionesstree
fathermotherpencil
brothersisterhouse
husbandwifecar

Ways to Form Feminine Nouns

WayMasculineFeminine
Add -esslionlioness
Add -ess (drop vowel)actoractress
Use different wordkingqueen
Add woman/girlpolicemanpolicewoman

💡 Remember: Neuter nouns are for non-living things and plants. Animals and people have gender.

Summary Table

TopicKey PointsExample
Proper NounSpecific name, capital letterRam, Nepal
Common NounGeneral nameboy, city
Collective NounGroup nameteam, army
Material NounSubstance namegold, wood
Abstract NounIdea/quality namehonesty
SingularOnebook
PluralMore than onebooks
CountableCan be countedapple
UncountableCannot be countedmilk
MasculineMaleking
FeminineFemalequeen
NeuterNo gendertable

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Identify the Noun Type

Read each sentence and write what type of noun is underlined.

  1. Nepal is a beautiful country. (Proper / Common / Abstract)
  2. The team played well. (Collective / Material / Proper)
  3. Honesty is important. (Abstract / Common / Collective)
  4. The ring is made of gold. (Material / Proper / Abstract)
  5. The boy ran fast. (Common / Proper / Collective)

Exercise 2: Singular to Plural

Change these singular nouns to plural.

SingularPlural
book______
child______
mango______
baby______
tooth______

Exercise 3: Countable or Uncountable?

Write C for countable or U for uncountable.

  1. milk ______
  2. pen ______
  3. rice ______
  4. apple ______
  5. water ______

Exercise 4: Fill in the Gender

Complete the pairs.

MasculineFeminine
king______
______mother
lion______
boy______
husband______

Exercise 5: Fill in the Blanks

Choose the correct word from the box.

(team, water, Kathmandu, happiness, books)

  1. The capital of Nepal is ____________.
  2. The cricket ____________ won the trophy.
  3. Please drink a glass of ____________.
  4. ____________ is more important than money.
  5. I have ten ____________ in my bag.

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. Proper, 2. Collective, 3. Abstract, 4. Material, 5. Common

Exercise 2: books, children, mangoes, babies, teeth

Exercise 3: 1. U, 2. C, 3. U, 4. C, 5. U

Exercise 4: queen, father, lioness, girl, wife

Exercise 5: 1. Kathmandu, 2. team, 3. water, 4. Happiness, 5. books

Great work! You can now name everything around you. In the next unit, we will learn how to replace nouns with pronouns.

🎯
